learning_outcomes.description: Apply occupational therapy models to identify the elements of the person, environment and occupation which change across the lifespan, and explain how these changes are linked with occupational performance in occupational roles.
Explain how life circumstances (including health conditions and environmental factors - historical and current) impact occupational performance and roles.
Assess aspects of occupational performance following the protocol and scoring instructions of a standardised developmental assessment tool, and interpret findings of this assessment.
Work collaboratively with peers to analyse and assess occupational performance.

Convey the outcomes of the assessment of occupational performance to relevant stakeholders both verbally and in a written format, using professional language and expression appropriate to the audience.

Apply, demonstrate and evaluate strategies related to communicating openly, effectively and respectfully with peers and professional colleagues; as well as demonstrate skills and strategies related to working collaboratively with colleagues; and with relevant stakeholders.

assessments.assessment_title: Occupational Performance Summary Report AND Prac (fieldwork simulation)
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to participate in both practical classes in Weeks 2 and 3. During these sessions, students will engage in an authentic clinical learning experience involving a paediatric case study and the scoring of a standardised occupational therapy assessment.


Through participation in these practical classes, students will develop skills in:

  • Assessing occupational performance in early childhood
  • Administering a standardised assessment
  • Scoring and interpreting assessment findings
  • Communicating assessment outcomes using occupational therapy terminology


The practical classes provide the assessment data, clinical information, and learning experiences required to complete Assessment Task 1, a written Occupational Performance Summary Report. Knowledge developed from participating in the practical sessions will also contribute to Assessment Task 2, the accompanying Oral Viva.

These practical classes simulate authentic occupational therapy practice and provide opportunities to develop assessment, communication, and clinical reasoning skills. Students are expected to actively engage in both sessions, as the assessment-related information and learning activities provided during class will not be replicated elsewhere. The practical classes also contribute to students' practice-based learning experiences in preparation for the 1,000 hours of practice education required for graduation and professional registration.

Due to client privacy and confidentiality requirements, materials such as video footage and clinical information will not be made available outside of class.


Written Report (Paired)

Working in pairs, students will prepare a professional occupational therapy report based on the paediatric case study and assessment information provided during the practical classes. The report will require students to communicate assessment findings and demonstrate their understanding of the child's occupational performance through the interpretation and analysis of assessment results and clinical information.


The Written Report will be worth 35% of your overall mark and will be submitted as a pair via Learn.uq by 2pm Thursday Week 6. 


If a student does not receive a pass mark for their first submission, they will be eligible to resubmit the task. However, any approved resubmission will result in a maximum achievable mark of a pass (55%).


Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Given this assessment is related to a real client please see more information on use of AI in student placements under additional assessment information. Students must clearly reference any use of AI in each instance using the AI declaration form. A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Report is to be submitted via Learn.uq. Only one individual from the group/pair required to upload assessment item.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
The Oral Viva will take place in Week 9 and will be completed individually via Zoom. Students will be required to discuss their written report with a ‘clinical educator’ and/or parent (examiner), demonstrating their ability to clinically reason and communicate assessment findings.

Please note:

  • Attendance and participation in the practical sessions are integral, as they directly inform the content assessed in the oral viva.
  • This viva will be recorded via Zoom. When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. (Assessment Procedure 95)


Additional details and guidance for this assessment task will be available in the Assessment Folder on Learn.UQ.


The Oral Viva will be worth 25% of your overall mark and is completed individually.


This assessment task is to be completed in person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This exam will take place during the end of semester examination period. It will cover content included throughout weeks 6 -13. The exam will be case based, with short answer questions. 

Note: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

Weekly pre-recorded lectures (approximately 2 hours per week) provide foundational knowledge across a range of early-stage support and management topics. Lectures are not timetabled and will be made available via blackboard ultra each Monday for flexible viewing. Students are expected to engage with the lecture content prior to attending their scheduled tutorial, where concepts will be applied through discussion, case-based learning, and practical activities.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Wednesday Tutorials

Tutorials will be held in person on campus during Weeks 1-13 of the semester. Each session will run for 2 hours and will incorporate inquiry-based learning and small group discussions to explore key concepts in greater depth. These sessions are designed as experiential learning activities, and participants are expected to attend and actively engage. To prepare, participants must complete view the lecture and complete any designated preparation tasks before each tutorial. As these sessions involve small group work, they will not be recorded.

In Week 12, tutorials will be replaced by a combined lived experience panel. This session will provide students with the opportunity to hear firsthand perspectives and further explore the application of theoretical concepts to introduced in the course.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 3(03 Aug - 16 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Prac Sessions (Weeks 2&3 only)

Attendance at both practical sessions is expected, as these classes contribute to the 1,000 hours of practice-based learning required for graduation and professional registration.

  • In weeks 2 and 3 students will attend two 2 hours in person practical sessions on campus. Students be introduced to a case study of a young child, which will form the basis for the developmental summary report and oral viva.
  •  As this is a small-group, interactive experience, it will not be recorded.


Please note:

  • These sessions provide essential hands-on experience and are foundational to the successful completion of the associated assessment tasks.


Recording of video materials is strictly prohibited.


Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06
